nodes.label: Threat Model: Unauthenticated Device API
nodes.rationale: httpapi.asp has no authentication — anyone reaching the device IP fully controls it, including destructive commands — and serves a self-signed cert; therefore the device is treated as trusted-LAN-only and all access sits behind the app's own auth.

nodes.label: HMAC-hashed Server Sessions
nodes.rationale: Only the HMAC (keyed by AUTH_SECRET) of the random 32-byte session token is stored in SQLite, so a DB leak alone cannot forge sessions.

nodes.label: Per-IP + Global Login Rate Limiting
nodes.rationale: A global cap exists in addition to the per-IP limit so spoofing X-Forwarded-For to rotate the apparent IP cannot grant unlimited attempts; failed logins are timing-equalised against a dummy hash to avoid user enumeration.

nodes.label: Nonce-based CSP + Security Headers
nodes.rationale: strict-dynamic nonce CSP with no unsafe-inline scripts; HSTS and upgrade-insecure-requests are disabled when COOKIE_SECURE=false for plain-http LAN testing.

nodes.label: Destructive Commands Not Proxied
nodes.rationale: reboot, shutdown, factory reset and network config are intentionally not implemented/proxied for safety.
